title: ICM and FS Sign Agreement for Fourth Greenfield Ethanol Plant in Brazil
content: ICM, Inc. has signed a major Professional Services and Equipment Purchase Agreement with FS IndÃºstria de Etanol S.A. to design and supply FSâ€™s fourth greenfield ethanol plant in Mato Grosso, Brazil, marking a milestone in the countryâ€™s renewable energy growth. Located near Campo Novo do Parecis and scheduled for completion in December 2026, the state-of-the-art dry-mill grain ethanol facility will have a production capacity of 540 million liters annually, making it FSâ€™s largest and most advanced project yet. The plant is set to create a significant economic boost for the region, generating around 600 direct jobs and 8,000 indirect jobs during construction, while also delivering valuable coproducts including 350,000 metric tons of DDGS for animal feed, 69,000 metric tons of corn oil, and 56,000 MWh of renewable electricity each year. ICM will integrate its cutting-edge technologies such as the Base Tricanter Systemâ„¢ for corn oil recovery, Selective Milling Technologyâ„¢ for optimized starch conversion, and Fiber Separation Technologyâ„¢ to increase ethanol yield and feed value, ensuring high efficiency and sustainability. Building on successful collaborations at FSâ€™s plants in Lucas do Rio Verde, Sorriso, and Primavera do Leste, this project strengthens the long-term partnership between FS and ICM and underscores their shared commitment to renewable fuels, energy security, and sustainable economic development in Brazilâ€™s agriculture-rich Mato Grosso region.
author: Analysts Account
